annotations.label: Triangle inequality (lengths)

anchor.snippet: the sum of the lengths of any two sides must be greater than or equal to the length of the remaining side

annotations.note: The geometric triangle inequality on side lengths is the metric-space inequality dist x z ≤ dist x y + dist y z, which holds in any (pseudo)metric space including Euclidean point sets.

annotations.label: Triangle inequality for norms

anchor.snippet: the triangle inequality is a theorem about vectors and vector lengths

annotations.note: norm_add_le states ‖a + b‖ ≤ ‖a‖ + ‖b‖, the triangle inequality for vector norms.

annotations.label: Shortest distance is a straight line

anchor.snippet: the shortest distance between two points is a straight line

annotations.note: dist_le_range_sum_dist shows the straight-line distance is a lower bound for any polygonal path, but the general curve-minimization statement is only partially captured.

annotations.label: Triangle inequality in spherical geometry

anchor.snippet: the triangle inequality holds provided the restriction is made

annotations.note: No spherical-geometry triangle inequality (for arc lengths/angles of spherical triangles) was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euclid's Elements I.20

anchor.snippet: the sum of any two sides is greater than the remaining one

annotations.note: Euclid I.20 is the triangle inequality on distances, formalized generally as dist_triangle for metric spaces.

annotations.label: Three inequalities form

anchor.section: Mathematical expression of the constraint on the sides of a triangle
anchor.snippet: literally translates into three inequalities

annotations.note: The three side inequalities a+b>c, b+c>a, c+a>b are just three applications of dist_triangle; no dedicated decl encodes this triple form.

annotations.label: Succinct form of inequality system

anchor.section: Mathematical expression of the constraint on the sides of a triangle
anchor.snippet: A more succinct form of this inequality system can be shown to be

annotations.note: The compact |a−b| < c < a+b form combines the triangle inequality (norm_add_le) and reverse triangle inequality (norm_sub_norm_le), but no single decl states it for triangle sides.

annotations.label: Longest side less than semiperimeter

anchor.section: Mathematical expression of the constraint on the sides of a triangle
anchor.snippet: the longest side length is less than the semiperimeter

annotations.note: No Mathlib declaration states that the longest side of a triangle is less than the semiperimeter.

annotations.label: Area positivity (Heron's formula)

anchor.section: Mathematical expression of the constraint on the sides of a triangle
anchor.snippet: the area of a triangle with sides a , b , c must be a real number greater than zero

annotations.note: Heron's formula appears only in Archive/Wiedijk100Theorems/HeronsFormula.lean, not in Mathlib/, and the area-positivity criterion is not formalized.

annotations.label: Golden ratio constraints

anchor.section: Mathematical expression of the constraint on the sides of a triangle
anchor.snippet: The triangle inequality provides two more interesting constraints for triangles whose sides are a , b , c

annotations.note: The golden-ratio bounds on geometric-progression triangle sides are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Right triangle specialization

anchor.snippet: the hypotenuse is greater than either of the two sides and less than their sum

annotations.note: No dedicated decl states the hypotenuse-versus-leg inequalities for right triangles, though they follow from dist_triangle and Pythagoras.

annotations.label: Sharpening by Pythagoras

anchor.snippet: is sharpened by Pythagoras' theorem to the equality

annotations.note: The Pythagorean theorem is formalized as an iff characterizing the right-angle case, of which the article's equality c²=a²+b² is one direction.

annotations.label: Arithmetic progression triangle

anchor.snippet: Consider a triangle whose sides are in an arithmetic progression

annotations.note: This worked example about arithmetic-progression triangle sides is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geometric progression triangle

anchor.snippet: Now consider a triangle whose sides are in a geometric progression

annotations.note: This worked example about geometric-progression triangle sides is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Polygon inequality

anchor.snippet: the total length of such a path is no less than the length of the straight line between its endpoints

annotations.note: dist_le_range_sum_dist gives dist (f 0) (f n) ≤ ∑ dist (f i) (f (i+1)), the polygon/path inequality for any number of intermediate points in a metric space.

annotations.label: Geometric progression quadrilateral

anchor.section: Example of the generalized polygon inequality for a quadrilateral
anchor.snippet: Consider a quadrilateral whose sides are in a geometric progression

annotations.note: This worked quadrilateral example is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Shortest curve is a straight line

anchor.snippet: the shortest curve between two points in Euclidean geometry is a straight line

annotations.note: riemannianEDist_le_pathELength shows the geodesic (Riemannian) distance lower-bounds any C¹ path length, capturing the curve-minimization idea but not the specific Euclidean straight-line characterization.

annotations.label: Converse of triangle inequality

anchor.snippet: if three real numbers are such that each is less than the sum of the others, then there exists a triangle

annotations.note: No Mathlib decl establishes existence of a triangle (or its embedding in the plane) from three lengths satisfying the strict inequalities.

annotations.label: Tetrahedron face inequality

anchor.snippet: The area of a triangular face of a tetrahedron is less than or equal to the sum of the areas of the other three triangular faces

annotations.note: The tetrahedral face-area inequality is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Polytope facet inequality

anchor.snippet: the inequality for a simplex of any dimension generalizes to a polytope of any dimension

annotations.note: The general facet-measure inequality for simplices/polytopes is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tetrahedral inequality strength

anchor.snippet: the tetrahedral inequality is stronger than several applications of the triangle inequality

annotations.note: This comparative remark about tetrahedral versus triangle inequalities is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Norm triangle inequality

anchor.snippet: one of the defining properties of the norm is the triangle inequality

annotations.note: The norm's subadditivity axiom is provided as norm_add_le : ‖a + b‖ ≤ ‖a‖ + ‖b‖.

annotations.label: Equality in strictly convex spaces

anchor.snippet: the triangle formed by u , v , and u + v , is degenerate

annotations.note: sameRay_iff_norm_add states ‖u+v‖ = ‖u‖+‖v‖ holds iff u and v are on the same ray (the degenerate case) in a strictly convex space.

annotations.label: Absolute value norm

anchor.snippet: The absolute value is a norm for the real line

annotations.note: Real.norm_eq_abs : ‖r‖ = |r| confirms the absolute value is the norm on ℝ.

annotations.label: Taxicab norm

anchor.snippet: The taxicab norm or 1-norm is one generalization absolute value to higher dimensions

annotations.note: The taxicab/1-norm is the PiLp p=1 instance, whose value PiLp.norm_eq_sum gives as the sum of coordinate norms.

anchors.snippet: The taxicab norm or 1-norm is one generalization absolute value to higher dimensions

annotations.label: Euclidean norm

anchor.snippet: The Euclidean norm or 2-norm defines the length of translation vectors

annotations.note: EuclideanSpace.norm_eq gives the 2-norm as the square root of the sum of squared coordinate norms.

anchors.snippet: The Euclidean norm or 2-norm defines the length of translation vectors

annotations.label: Triangle inequality from Cauchy–Schwarz

anchor.snippet: The triangle inequality follows from the Cauchy–Schwarz inequality

annotations.note: Mathlib derives the inner-product-space norm's triangle inequality (norm_add_le) precisely from Cauchy–Schwarz (norm_inner_le_norm).

annotations.label: Cauchy–Schwarz equality condition

anchor.snippet: The Cauchy–Schwarz inequality turns into an equality if and only if u and v are linearly dependent

annotations.note: norm_inner_eq_norm_iff states ‖⟪x,y⟫‖ = ‖x‖‖y‖ iff y is a nonzero scalar multiple of x, i.e. the vectors are linearly dependent.

annotations.label: Minkowski's inequality (p-norm)

anchor.snippet: The triangle inequality for general values of p is called Minkowski's inequality

annotations.note: Real.Lp_add_le is Minkowski's inequality: (∑|f+g|^p)^(1/p) ≤ (∑|f|^p)^(1/p) + (∑|g|^p)^(1/p) for p ≥ 1.

anchors.snippet: The triangle inequality for general values of p is called Minkowski's inequality

annotations.label: Metric triangle inequality

anchor.snippet: the triangle inequality is a requirement upon distance

annotations.note: dist_triangle : dist x z ≤ dist x y + dist y z is the metric-space triangle-inequality axiom.

annotations.label: Convergent sequences are Cauchy

anchor.snippet: any convergent sequence in a metric space is a Cauchy sequence is a direct consequence of the triangle inequality

annotations.note: Filter.Tendsto.cauchySeq shows a sequence tending to a limit is a Cauchy sequence (in any uniform space, generalizing the metric case).

annotations.label: Reverse triangle inequality

anchor.snippet: Any side of a triangle is greater than or equal to the difference between the other two sides

annotations.note: abs_norm_sub_norm_le : |‖a‖ − ‖b‖| ≤ ‖a − b‖ is the reverse triangle inequality.

annotations.label: Lipschitz continuity of the norm

anchor.snippet: are Lipschitz continuous with Lipschitz constant 1

annotations.note: lipschitzWith_one_norm states the norm map is LipschitzWith 1, a direct consequence of the reverse triangle inequality.

annotations.label: Cosine similarity triangle inequality

anchor.snippet: By applying the cosine function to the triangle inequality and reverse triangle inequality for arc lengths

annotations.note: The triangle inequality for cosine similarity is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Reversed inequality in Minkowski space

anchor.snippet: if u and v are both timelike vectors lying in the future light cone , the triangle inequality is reversed

annotations.note: The reversed triangle inequality for timelike vectors in Minkowski space is not formalized in Mathlib.
